entries.name: Keep my soldiers alive — ninjō vs the orders that spend them
entries.note: The engine of the drama. His cohort carries a FALCON (Toritaka) contingent — clansmen serving the Legion under their famous-blooded son; his ninjō is to march them home to the Valley of the Spirits ALIVE. Every order that spends lives to win the Emperor's war pokes it. The cruelty: his giri (honor the Falcon) is paid in the same Falcon lives his ninjō would save. Trigger to watch: Softheartedness (Fire) — he seizes up about KILLING, +3 strife when he takes a life. (Also Fear of Mediocrity (Fire): +3 strife when he must improvise/adapt to the unexpected on a Fire check.)

entries.name: The black-sheep blood & uncle Seppun Tadanobu's leash
entries.note: His known Imperial blood comes from the scandalous branch — grandfather left the Seppun to marry a Toritaka; uncle Tadanobu redeemed it and sponsors Akihiko. The favor is a leash: what does the uncle expect in return, and who resents the upjumped black-sheep nephew?

entries.name: Officers who know whose nephew he is — expectation & resentment
entries.note: Akihiko serves under officers aware of his Seppun patron and his minor-clan origins. ELEVATION here is rolled, not narrated (Status-priced Fate Questions); trust and favor arrive with strings.

entries.name: The spirit-dark of the battlefield — what the Falcon sees that the war hides
entries.note: Quiet thread. A battlefield is a feast for the dead; Akihiko's Falcon spirit-sight (Sixth Sense) may find yūrei, gaki, or worse moving under cover of the mundane war. Latent — let the dice surface it.

entries.name: The buried truth — known, sealed, undrawable
entries.note: ARC-2 KEY (bumped to w3 — now the literal lever of the oath to restore Kasumi). The full proof of the Lion's poison+looting (manifest + Throne-seals from Shizue's brazier + Ume's dated ledger + Crane+Lion depositions) is sealed 'at the highest level' to stop a Right-vs-Left court war. UNDRAWABLE in a testimony-bound court — EXCEPT before the Dragon's KITSUKI, who weigh physical evidence, and whose NEUTRALITY toward the Lion makes a finding stick (unlike a Crane's dismissable word). Drawing it here = un-burying the verdict AND recasting Kasumi as the Matsu who felled a proven traitor (the oath's key). Open questions: who keeps the sealed key; can Akihiko get the evidence to a Kitsuki venue; the Lion's quiet debt vs their desperation to keep it buried; the risk that reopening it makes him (and Kasumi) targets again.

entries.name: The matriarch's grant — Chiyo, a rōnin bride, and the family rift
entries.note: ARC-2 ROMANCE + FAMILY thread (supersedes 'The shut gate' after the marriage-authority canon fix). Tadanobu (Seppun, married OUT) holds no say over a Toritaka match — only disfavor; he refused (Fate: No). The real authority, grandmother Toritaka CHIYO (iron daimyō, head of the line), GRANTED leave (Fate: 50/50 → 12 → YES) — WITH STRINGS: (1) she'll judge the rōnin Kasumi in person (a scene to play); (2) she'd adopt Kasumi into the Falcon vassal house to restore a name — binding Kasumi to Chiyo, indebted, and winning the house a famous Matsu duelist; (3) Akihiko's primary loyalty pulled HOME to her Falcon house, away from the Seppun uncle. Net: the romance is ALIVE on a clanless bride's terms, and a quiet GRANDMOTHER-vs-UNCLE rift opens with Akihiko the prize. Live levers: the matriarch's audience with Kasumi; the adoption's price; navigating the two relatives; restoring enough of Kasumi's face that the match honors the house.

entries.name: The Scattered Corps — silent on the line
entries.note: ARC-2 DORMANT thread (Fate: Likely → 30 → NO — none surfaced at Arc-1's close). Akihiko's real friends/colleagues — the cross-clan healers and wardens bound to him by a past crisis they survived together (see roster/setting-canon). At the largest moment of his life — the conspiracy broken, the vow kept before an army — NOT ONE was there, and word has reached none of them; war keeps them scattered across half of Rokugan on their own clans' duties. The bond is real but dark; no letter waits, no familiar face turns up. A warm distant ache, latent: one may surface when the reassignment lands him somewhere new (the dice or the player can wake it). For now: accounted for, alive as far as he knows, unreachable.

entries.name: The Dragonfly posting — Imperial referee at the Dragon/Phoenix frontier
entries.note: ARC-2 STAGE (the reassignment resolved here; the Legion's brush, not Akihiko's choice). Posted to the sensitive DRAGON↔PHOENIX frontier — the Tonbo (Dragonfly) Minor Clan, 'gatekeepers to the Dragon,' beside a key Phoenix court (Pale Oak Castle, a Hantei winter-court favorite). The Imperial referee's job: keep the peace in a border dispute. WHY IT MATTERS FOR THE OATH: this is Kitsuki country — the Dragon's evidence-based justice is the ONE tradition that weighs physical proof over testimony, and the Dragon are NEUTRAL to the Lion (no enmity) → a Kitsuki finding can't be dismissed as enemy malice the way a Crane's word was. The single posting that can un-bury the truth in a Lion-undismissable form. Also: shugenja/Phoenix country (the sun-priest's element + the spirit-dark thread thrives), a minor clan (Tonbo) for the Falcon's sympathy, and Reiko's court-world follows. NB: Akihiko's core Falcon contingent transfers with him (his ninjō rides along). To FRAME/open as Arc 2's first scene.

entries.name: The oath to restore Kasumi — un-disinherit the Matsu, reclaim her castle
entries.note: ARC-2 SPINE (sworn at Arc-1's close; Honor +1 trifling for the swearing — the MAJOR Honor is reserved for keeping it). Akihiko swore a binding oath to get the LION (the Matsu) to REVERSE Kasumi's disinheritance — restore her name, her clan, and her ancestral castle/holding — rather than settle for Chiyo adopting her into the Falcon house. A far harder, more honorable goal: forcing the Great Clan that cast her out to take her back. THE KEY is the buried truth: only a Lion-UNDISMISSABLE vindication (a NEUTRAL Dragon/Kitsuki evidence-finding — see that thread) recasts her from 'rogue who murdered a commander' to 'the loyal Matsu who lawfully felled a PROVEN traitor,' giving the Lion grounds + pressure to restore her. TEETH (like the vow to the dead): forsworn/failed = a MAJOR Honor forfeit, the romance likely lost (she stays rōnin/Falcon-vassal at best), the Lion made permanent enemies. FRICTION: this denies grandmother Chiyo her prize (she granted the match to win a famous duelist for HER banner; restoring Kasumi to the Lion sends the bride back to a rival Great Clan) — the Chiyo-adoption becomes the SAFETY NET, not the goal.

entries.name: The Toshi Ranbo war — grinding on offscreen
entries.note: BACKGROUND now (Akihiko posted away to the Dragonfly border). The Lion–Crane war over Toshi Ranbo grinds on without him; one commander dying honest changed nothing for the armies. Stays live because the LION (his oath's target) are fighting it, the buried truth concerns it, and Sukune/the Crane are in it. Latent: news from the front, a shift in the war, or the Lion's posture toward the buried scandal can reach him at the Dragonfly frontier.
